item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: short, simple, and quality. very good. interesting idea without overcomplication

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do I feel the Foundation did a very stupid thing by removing that etched concrete? Obviously this thing was built, as it was, for a reason. Including possibly magic runes of sealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to imagine this would be somehow connected to 2317.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like my scps with at least a sliver of possible reality. This has none of it. the containment procedures seem woefully inadequate, as anyone in a space station might see this, costing the foundation a ton more in amnestics than it makes it seem like, unless the chain magically dissapears after it leaves a certain height, which it says nothing about. I like the under the earth portion, but the upper end ruins it for me. Downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest this seems too much like 144 in my opinion. Yeah, this is anchoring something as opposed to leading somewhere, but the basic concept is the same. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. The biggest reason being that someone hasn't done it yet, and it's such a simple thing. You didn't add too much (it's obvious from the lack of details that it doesn't cross the moon's orbit, etc) but it still beats the thumb-length rule. This is one of those articles that just feels like it IS something the Foundation would have on file, and that's why I can +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really lame/awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have posted the reason behind my +1 quite succinctly. Thank you sir/madam/other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After thinking about it for a while, I've realized that the anchor starts at 2 meters below surface, and continues down the length of chain, however deep that may be. But my first impression was of a cinder-block sized object that gave the chain a definite, 2 meters deep, Earth side terminator. That obviously doesn't mesh with the rest of the text. I think I got this mistaken impression from "excavated", which to me implies the whole thing was dug-up. Maybe toss a "partially" in front of there, or say that the first 5-10 meters have been uncovered.

Of course, I might just need more caffeine and be the only one with the problem, so it's your call.

Good piece. I'm curous about what's being restrained, and anything that provokes my curiousity gets a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man. Even if this was simply a chain extending into the heavens that didn't let you look too closely at it, it would be worth an upvote. The extra stuff is in some ways not needed, but it's also handled in a good enough way to leave questions and be intriguing. Good work, a nice, clean article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He made it himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. That. Also, why was there bacon in the soap?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: does this mean...?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also a valid (and awesome) interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thought that this chain is holding something never really occurred to me. I got the thought that something was holding us. Kind of like an intergalactic towing service, dragging our poor solar system off somewhere, with the Earth as a sort of anchor point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*shakes fist*

FRAAAAAAAAAANCE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, "meter" is the American spelling. "Metre" is the spelling everywhere else uses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chain links are roughly sixty centimetres in diameter

I'm still getting used to the metric system, but wouldn't it be centimeters?

Any grammatical errors aside, this really is a good article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This here is really fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Classic and classy.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of one of the older articles, but in a good way. Short, sweet and intriguing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal favourite “what is this thing” simple article is Josie. An axolotl and a bullet also spring to mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if this is the best example, but I believe Drewbear's Candle of Life fits that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please list more articles that have that perfect amount of "what the hell is this thing and why's it there?!" and simplicity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It makes me think, what the hell is under there that needs restraining so much? And why is the chain so damn big?

Unless the thing that's being restrained is on the other end of the chain, beyond the moon's orbit… Now that's intriguing. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has the perfect amount of 'what the hell is this thing, and why's it there?

This. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess that's why the Foundation contains it, now isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, okay. I was confused on that bit. and I agree that you should make it a bit more obvious that it's holding something, instead of hinting at it. No need for some crazy "HOLY SHIT SPACE DEMONS IN CHAINS" thing, but something needs to be done about that. Also, whatever it's holding, can that really reliably be held by a 2-ft wide chain? and why would it need to be held so far from the earth? And where would the (presumably) ancient Greenlanders get the super-iron that's holding this thing down, much less enough to hold a (minimum) 406 thousand kilometer, 2 foot thick chain? This is making me ask so many questions, which I think is a double-edged sword XD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chain does not cross the path of the moon's orbit. It is merely long enough that it sticks out beyond it. It would not pass directly in front of or behind the moon at any point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never mind planes, which the foundation could divert, in theory: if this thing extends into the path of the moon's orbit, how has it not been struck by the moon, or at the very least, whatever's on the end shown up on the face of it? Perhaps the foundation moon base would be capable of observing it? I know cross-scp's are bad ideas most of the time, but the lunar base would be an interesting way to go at it, I think… though I would probably save that for a tale. Just a thought. No vote for now til I mull it over some more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Swingin' a chain, swingin' a chain, swingin' a chain…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have tried to kill two birds with one stone regarding this with an additional sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The simplicity is appealing to me, I feel lots of people nowadays feel they have to create a really complex and long-winded SCP, with interviews, addendums and incident reports etc. This SCP reminds me of the old days, and it has the perfect amount of 'what the hell is this thing, and why's it there?' Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just feels incomplete to me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah there's nothing interesting here or everyhting is too subtle for my brain at 4:30 AM.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no hook, and there's really no way to fix that. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:- /
This could have been something I liked. But the effect isn't interesting on its own, the purpose isn't clear enough for me to 'get it', and having read the comments the "restrain something" idea isn't expressed in the article at all.
And the worst part… we should be able to track this far, far beyond the moon's orbit. Aim a radar/laser beam of a certain frequency at it, look/listen for a reflection, and you should be able detect it from a distance of several AU, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote. This is interesting and along the lines of the kind of Ophite SCPs I like, but the hook isn't strong enough.

Also dwarven megaconstructiun lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Foundation has reworked air routes around it, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lemme poke at that a bit. I did take that into account in the original version, but apparently not clearly enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Maybe not very often, but some will eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do many planes cross over Greenland?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not going to vote. Maybe I'm over-thinking things, but, how would a plane not run into this at some point?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally it was just a chain in both directions. However, I realized it would quickly wear away the earth around it with the contraction/tension cycle. This also gave me the lock thing; this runs the risk of being anomalous object long if I cut that, and without it there really isn't anything indicating the purpose of the chain (to restrain something); it's just a long-ass chain that keeps falling down at that point.

Also, they know how far it goes out into the atmosphere. The problem is it goes clean out into space… and just keeps going. Their lower limit through direct observation is lunar orbit. As I said, it's a long-ass chain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I think that this would be better without the block of dense stuff underground. Just a chain that goes up forever and down forever.

However, there are ways of measuring something that goes up into the atmosphere. Unless this was written in the fourth century or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. We'll see where the votes land on this one, and I'll be sure to reread through the archive again before vomiting forth another SCP from my cavernous maw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, yeah, I'm not saying that the two don't have different ideas behind them, I just don't think that this one is sufficiently different and well-executed to overcome their similarities. Keep writing, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better. Short and sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Also, I totally forgot about that SCP existing, which is weird because it was probably one of the first ones I ever read back when I first stumbled on SCP. I think the themes diverge enough, though. SCP-144 is a means to transport yourself to places unknown. This is intended to restrain something unknown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's material

And formatting/grammar aside, it reminds me too much of SCP-144, which is far cooler from a thematic standpoint.

The writing here seems to be decent, so you're at least on the right track.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Particularly how short and simple you left it, without unnecessary bits and bobs thrown in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but I can't in good conscience upvote an article with bad formatting and its-it's errors.

Proofread this and we'll see.
